topics.guiding_question: Tell me about how your pregnancy has been so far.
objectives: Understand the participant's overall emotional and physical experience of pregnancy
Identify any major events, turning points, or surprises during the pregnancy
Establish rapport and get a general sense of the participant's context

topics.guiding_question: I am curious what you've been eating during your pregnancy. Tell me about what you ate throughout the day yesterday. What are your thoughts about what you should eat and drink during pregnancy?
objectives: Understand the participant's actual dietary behaviours and patterns during pregnancy
Explore beliefs, knowledge, and attitudes about what constitutes a healthy pregnancy diet
Identify barriers or enablers to eating well (e.g. cravings, nausea, access, cost)

topics.guiding_question: What are your thoughts about what you should do for exercise during pregnancy?
objectives: Understand the participant's beliefs and knowledge about safe exercise during pregnancy
Explore actual exercise habits and how they have changed since becoming pregnant
Identify motivators, barriers, and concerns related to physical activity

topics.guiding_question: Your body changes a lot during pregnancy. What have you noticed changing? How do you feel?
objectives: Understand the participant's awareness of and feelings about physical body changes
Explore attitudes toward pregnancy weight gain, including any anxiety or acceptance
Identify social or cultural influences on how the participant perceives her body

topics.guiding_question: We want to know how to help women be healthy during their pregnancy. What would have helped you to make healthy choices during your pregnancy?
objectives: Identify gaps in support, information, or resources the participant experienced
Understand what types of interventions or resources the participant would find useful
Capture concrete suggestions grounded in the participant's own experience

topics.guiding_question: Thank you for taking the time to tell me a little bit about your pregnancy. Is there anything else you think I should know?
objectives: Give the participant space to share anything not yet covered
Confirm that the most important themes have been captured
Close the interview on a positive, respectful note
